description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 7 data & retrieval cluster: ingestion/training integrity (poisoning) vs retrieval authorization vs third-party artifact acquisition vs inference-time injection, and against the shipped supply-chain-security-reviewer.

cases.prompt: Can an attacker corrupt what our model learns by getting crafted data into our fine-tuning set or our feedback loop?
cases.reason: Integrity of training/feedback data you run is this skill (LLM04); rag-security-architect handles retrieval permission and supply-chain-security-reviewer handles acquired artifacts.

cases.prompt: Make sure tenant A can't retrieve tenant B's documents from our shared vector index.
cases.reason: Who can retrieve which vectors is rag-security-architect (LLM08); this skill covers whether the ingested content itself is trustworthy, not query-time access.

cases.prompt: Audit the third-party base model and public dataset we downloaded for provenance and unsafe serialization before we use them.
cases.reason: Acquired models/datasets/adapters and their provenance/serialization are supply-chain-security-reviewer (LLM03, extended per D6); this skill owns data you ingest and pipelines you run.

cases.prompt: A retrieved document contains instructions that hijacked our agent at answer time — stop that.
cases.reason: Content acting as instructions at inference is prompt-injection-defender (LLM01); this skill covers content being indexed/trained as ground truth (integrity), not the injection vector.
